operating_state: Total revenue declined to $45.7 billion from $51.7 billion driven by 30% volume decreases in U.S. and Canada large agriculture, partially offset by growth in Brazil and India small agriculture markets. Agriculture backlog orders decreased year-over-year while Construction & Forestry backlog increased substantially, reflecting divergent end-market demand patterns across segments.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio deteriorated to 72.4% from 68.8% despite absolute cost reduction, reflecting production inefficiencies from lower volumes and direct tariff impact of approximately $600 million. Manufacturing operations focus on cost optimization through process improvements and supply management while selling and administrative expenses decreased 4% due to lower profit-sharing incentives and Banco John Deere deconsolidation impact.
material_events: Completed deconsolidation of Banco John Deere through 50% sale to Banco Bradesco for $362 million, retaining 50% equity interest as joint venture. Federal Trade Commission filed antitrust lawsuit alleging monopolization in equipment repair markets with preliminary settlement discussions ongoing. Company refined Leap Ambitions strategic framework emphasizing automation, digitalization, and Solutions as a Service through 2030.
macro_exposures: New U.S. tariffs created direct impact of approximately $600 million excluding supplier and demand effects, with Supreme Court hearing arguments regarding tariff authority and potential relief. Agricultural equipment demand remains sensitive to farm cash receipts, commodity prices, and government policies while construction equipment depends on infrastructure investment levels and broader economic conditions.
forward_outlook: Large agriculture sales in North America expected to remain subdued in 2026 while small agriculture, turf, and construction forestry sales expected to improve. Management executing Smart Industrial Operating Model with capital expenditures forecasted at $1.4 billion for 2026 while maintaining dividend growth and opportunistic share repurchases under existing authorization with $7.9 billion remaining.
uncertainty_profile: FTC antitrust lawsuit outcome remains unpredictable with accrued losses on unresolved legal matters totaling approximately $175 million. Credit loss allowances increased due to elevated delinquencies and declining market conditions in retail financing portfolio while customer adoption of precision technology and subscription services has been slower than expected, delaying anticipated benefits realization.
